assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain: Repository (e.g. 'trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections. Verified by reading each file: task-1 has Files to Create and Files to Modify; task-2 has Files to Modify; task-3 has Files to Create and Files to Modify; task-4 has Files to Modify; task-5 has Files to Create and Files to Modify; task-6 has Files to Modify.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 6 task files in the outputs directory: task-1-advisory-severity-model.md, task-2-advisory-severity-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-integration-tests.md, task-6-api-documentation.md. 6 is between 3 and 10 inclusive.
All 6 task files have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' as their Repository section. Verified in each file: task-1 line 4, task-2 line 4, task-3 line 4, task-4 line 4, task-5 line 4, task-6 line 4.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Tasks 4, 5, and 6 each depend on Task 3. This forms a tree/DAG structure: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> {4, 5, 6}. No cycles exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify paths all exist in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, tests/Cargo.toml, README.md. Files to Create paths are new files in existing directories from repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (in sbom/model/ alongside summary.rs and details.rs), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (in sbom/endpoints/ alongside list.rs and get.rs), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (in tests/api/ alongside sbom.rs and advisory.rs). All paths are plausible within the repository structure.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md lists 6 specific changes with concrete details: (1) 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model' with specific fields (critical, high, medium, low, total) and pattern reference (SbomSummary/SbomDetails); (2) 'Add advisory severity aggregation query to SbomService' mentioning sbom_advisory join table, deduplication, threshold parameter; (3) 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint' with specific path, 404 validation, 5-minute cache; (4) 'Add cache invalidation for advisory summary on advisory ingestion'; (5) 'Add integration tests' listing specific test scenarios; (6) 'Update REST API documentation' with specific items to document.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain a '## Repository' section with exactly one repository name 'trustify-backend' on a single line. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified by reading each file in full.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths map directly to paths in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (line 41), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (line 46), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (line 48), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (line 83), tests/Cargo.toml (line 114), README.md (line 5). Files to Create paths are new files placed in directories that exist in the manifest (sbom/model/, sbom/endpoints/, tests/api/), following the naming pattern of adjacent files.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs (SbomSummary)' and 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs (SbomDetails)', mentions specific derive macros. Task 2: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs' fetch/list methods, 'entity/src/sbom_advisory.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs'. Task 3: references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs', Axum Router pattern. Task 4: references 'modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs', 'modules/ingestor/src/service/mod.rs (IngestorService)'. Task 5: references 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', assert_eq! pattern. Task 6: references 'README.md' and existing documentation format.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files have '## Target Branch\nmain': task-1 line 7-8, task-2 line 7-8, task-3 line 7-8, task-4 line 7-8, task-5 line 7-8, task-6 line 7-8.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No sha256 digest comments or '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' markers were found anywhere in the outputs directory. grep for 'sha256', 'digest', and 'Description digest' across all output files returned zero matches. The output files contain only the task markdown content and impact map, with no digest comments.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention applicability rationales were found in any task file. grep for 'CONVENTIONS', 'convention', and 'Applies:' across all output files returned only one hit in task-3 line 28 which uses 'convention' in lowercase referring to a general coding convention ('following the convention where all handlers return Result<T, AppError>'), not a CONVENTIONS.md section reference. None of the task files contain 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' references, 'Applies: task modifies' rationale format, or any indication that convention-applicability-rules.md was applied. The repo-backend.md mentions a CONVENTIONS.md file exists in the repository, but no conventions were enriched into the task descriptions.
